title: new https.Agent with rejectUnauthorized: false — every request through this agent skips TLS validation (MITM)

params.passDetail: The https.Agent keeps certificate validation on (rejectUnauthorized is not false) — connections made through it verify the server's TLS identity.
params.failDetail: new https.Agent is created with rejectUnauthorized: false, disabling TLS certificate validation for EVERY request routed through this agent. When the agent is attached to axios/got/node-fetch (a common way to 'fix' a cert error globally), the whole HTTP client silently trusts forged certificates and can be man-in-the-middled. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; pass a proper `ca` if a private CA is required.
params.absentCallDetail: No https.Agent construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: https.Agent does not set rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (true) applies.

provenance.note: Setting rejectUnauthorized: false on a shared https.Agent is the 'global' version of the per-request bypass — one agent disables MITM protection for an entire HTTP client. Pasted from a Stack Overflow answer to silence a self-signed-cert error, it ships everywhere the agent is reused.
